purpose: Verify polylane-run fires on its 5 documented trigger phrases (and NOT on plan-only, auto-pipeline, or generic run/launch prompts), refuses to run without the manifest, always dry-runs before launch, resolves the runner portably, and relays the end-of-run report to the chat.
schema: Shared schema for all eval files documented in evals/README.md.

cases.name: trigger_slash_polylane_run
cases.input: /polylane-run
expect: polylane-run skill activates
checks `.polylane/run.json` exists before anything else

cases.name: trigger_run_the_lanes
cases.input: run the lanes
expect: polylane-run skill activates (NOT polylane-auto — plan already exists, only execution wanted)
resolves runner script, preflights tmux/jq/claude

cases.name: trigger_launch_the_terminals
cases.input: launch the terminals
expect: polylane-run skill activates
dry-run shown before any pane opens

cases.name: trigger_execute_the_plan
cases.input: execute the plan
expect: polylane-run skill activates
consumes only `.polylane/run.json` — no other input

cases.name: trigger_start_the_builders
cases.input: start the builders
expect: polylane-run skill activates
one tmux pane per lane per the manifest

cases.name: paraphrase_fire_up_planned_lanes
cases.input: the polylane plan is ready — fire up the tmux panes for it
expect: polylane-run skill activates (paraphrase of 'run the lanes' / 'launch the terminals')
dry-run first, launch only on go-ahead

cases.name: paraphrase_kick_off_manifest
cases.input: kick off the lanes that /polylane just planned
expect: polylane-run skill activates (paraphrase of 'start the builders')
drives bin/polylane-run.sh — does not plan or merge by hand

cases.name: negative_disambiguation_make_lane_prompts
cases.input: make lane prompts for these goals
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate — planning wanted, that is polylane's trigger

cases.name: negative_disambiguation_plan_and_run
cases.input: plan and run — do the whole thing in one go
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate — full pipeline (interview through merge) is polylane-auto's trigger; polylane-run only executes an existing plan

cases.name: negative_run_the_tests
cases.input: run the tests
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate (test execution, not lane launching)

cases.name: negative_start_dev_server
cases.input: start the dev server and launch the app in my browser
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate ('start'/'launch' about an app, no lanes/plan/builders context)

cases.name: negative_single_agent
cases.input: run one agent to fix this bug
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate (single agent, no planned lanes)

cases.name: negative_review_pr
cases.input: review my PR
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate (code review request)

cases.name: negative_install_skill
cases.input: install a skill for me
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate (skill install, not lane execution)

cases.name: negative_execute_script
cases.input: execute the migration script against staging
expect: polylane-run does NOT activate ('execute' about a DB script, not the lane plan)

cases.name: behavior_manifest_missing_stops
cases.input: /polylane-run in a repo with no .polylane/run.json
expect: stops and tells the user: no `.polylane/run.json`, run `/polylane` first
does NOT fabricate a manifest
opens zero panes

cases.name: behavior_preflight_tools
cases.input: /polylane-run on a machine missing jq
expect: preflights tmux, jq, claude on PATH
reports the missing tool with its install command (`brew install jq`) and stops
no launch attempt with missing tools

cases.name: behavior_dry_run_before_launch
cases.input: manifest present, tools ok — user says go
expect: ALWAYS runs `"$RUNNER" .polylane/run.json --dry-run` first and relays pane count + lane + model/effort table to the user
real launch (same command without --dry-run) only AFTER the user approves the dry-run output
never skips the dry-run even when the user sounds eager

cases.name: behavior_runner_resolved_portably
cases.input: /polylane-run invoked from a project that is not the polylane repo
expect: resolves the runner via PATH, falling back to `$HOME/.claude/skills/polylane/bin/polylane-run.sh`
never calls a bare relative `bin/polylane-run.sh`
`test -x "$RUNNER"` guard before use

cases.name: behavior_model_flags_compose_with_dry_run
cases.input: user wants backend lane pinned to Opus and the run at balanced intensity
expect: layers `--intensity balanced --model backend=claude-opus-4-8` onto the same runner call
previews the flag combination with --dry-run and reads back the per-lane model column before the real launch
does not edit the manifest to change models

cases.name: behavior_report_relayed_to_chat
cases.input: run finishes (GO or NO-GO)
expect: reads `docs/polylane-report.md` when the run completes
relays a simple plain-language summary in the chat (outcome, per-lane results, next steps)
explains the runner's autonomous behavior once at launch: auto-poll, auto-retry transient errors, auto-integrate, merge on GO, cleanup

cases.name: behavior_never_hand_merges
cases.input: integrator finishes and branches are ready
expect: the shell script owns integrate/merge/cleanup — the skill never edits source or merges by hand
skill's role ends at driving the runner and relaying its output

red_flags: fires on plan-only ('make lane prompts') or full-pipeline ('plan and run') prompts instead of deferring to polylane / polylane-auto
fails to fire on any of the 5 documented trigger phrases
fires on generic run/launch/start/execute prompts with no lane context
launches panes without a prior --dry-run review
calls bare relative `bin/polylane-run.sh` outside the polylane repo
edits source files or merges branches by hand
finishes without relaying `docs/polylane-report.md` to the chat
